seoTitle: Pay Sales Tax in QuickBooks Online (Step by Step)
metaDescription: Record and file sales tax payments in QuickBooks Online using the automated Sales Tax dashboard, from liability review to settlement.
intro: Tracking sales tax owed across jurisdictions gets complicated fast without a central dashboard. This guide shows how to use QuickBooks Online's Sales Tax center to review liabilities, record payments, and keep your books audit-ready.

steps.heading: Open the Sales Tax dashboard
steps.body: Go to Taxes in the left sidebar and select the Sales Tax tab to see your live tax agency overview.

steps.heading: Review your liability
steps.body: Find the specific tax agency row and check the outstanding balance under Due This Month.

steps.heading: View the return
steps.body: Click View Return to see gross sales, taxable sales, and total tax collected for the period.

steps.heading: Record the payment
steps.body: Click Record Payment, choose your payment date and funding bank account, then enter the payment amount.

steps.heading: Confirm and post
steps.body: Add a reference note like the filing period, then click Record Payment to push the balancing entry through your ledger.

faq.question: Where do I see how much sales tax I owe?
faq.answer: The Sales Tax dashboard under Taxes shows each agency's outstanding balance in the Due This Month column.

faq.question: Can I file my return directly through QuickBooks?
faq.answer: The module lets you review your return details and record the payment, clearing the corresponding liability balance.

faq.question: How do I verify past sales tax payments?
faq.answer: Run the Sales Tax Liability report to review historical tax data and keep an audit-ready paper trail.

faq.question: Which bank account should fund the tax payment?
faq.answer: Select your actual operating checking account so the payment matches your real-world bank withdrawal.
